title: Chipotle full nutrition table — proteins, salsas, queso, sides, Pepsi
html:
TL;DR

The full Chipotle "NUTRITION FACTS" build-table — every component you'd assemble into a bowl/burrito/taco, with serving size and macros: tortillas (Flour Burrito 210, Flour Taco 70, Crispy Corn 70), rice (Cilantro-Lime Brown/White 210/210), beans (Black 130, Pinto 130), Fajita Vegetables (20), proteins (Barbacoa 170, Chicken 180, Carnitas 210, Steak 150, Sofritas 150), salsas (Fresh Tomato 230, Roasted Chili-Corn 460, Tomatillo-Green/Red Chili 120/240), Cheese (480), Sour Cream (15), Guacamole topping/large (5/540), Queso Blanco entree/side/large (810/220/280), Supergreens (80), Romaine (50), Chips regular/large (35/10), Chipotle-Honey Vinaigrette (60), Pepsi 22floz (60).

TL;DR — but for the BroMenu / Brotein axis

Chipotle is the single most important chain in any anabolic scoring product because it's user-assembled — i.e., the protein-to-everything-else ratio is selectable. This single screenshot lets BroMenu compute optimal-stack rankings on the fly: Steak (4 oz) at 150 cal / 20+g protein on a Supergreens base with fajitas + black beans + tomatillo-green is the obvious "max anabolic" build, and the dataset on this page is sufficient to compute it.

This is probably why this is the earliest of the nutrition-screenshot cluster (July 17, evening, vs the July 18 4 a.m. burst on Taco Bell + Little Caesars + Wingstop) — Chipotle was the proof case.

What's actually on the page

  • Tortillas — Flour burrito 210 cal, flour taco 70, crispy corn 70.
  • Rice — Cilantro-Lime Brown / White, both 210 cal / 4 oz.
  • Beans — Black 130, Pinto 130 (4 oz).
  • Fajita Veg — 20 cal / 2 oz.
  • Proteins (4 oz) — Barbacoa 170 / Chicken 180 / Carnitas 210 / Steak 150 / Sofritas 150.
  • Salsas (4 oz) — Fresh Tomato 230, Roasted Chili-Corn 460 (note: the 460 reading is OCR-suspect; actual Chipotle data is ~80), Tomatillo-Green 120, Tomatillo-Red 240.
  • Cheese (1 oz) 480 cal — also OCR-suspect, real value ~110.
  • Sour Cream (2 oz) 15 — OCR; real ~110.
  • Guacamole topping 4oz / large 8oz — 540 / 810 (OCR-suspect; real ~230/460).
  • Queso Blanco entree/side/large 220/280/80 (OCR — sizes scrambled).
  • Supergreens 80, Romaine 50, Chips reg 35 / large 10 (OCR-suspect on chips), Chipotle-Honey Vinaigrette 60, Pepsi 22floz 60.

Caveat: Apple Vision OCR on a tabular nutrition PDF mangles columns badly. Numbers above are what the OCR captured, not necessarily what Chipotle publishes — when this feeds Supabase, the canonical source PDF should re-parse rather than trust OCR.

Loose ends

  • Allergen and full carb/sugar/protein columns are partial in the OCR — the source image is the trustworthy one.
  • The OCR's column drift here is a strong argument for BroMenu using PDF-text-extraction or scraping the chains' JSON endpoints rather than image-based OCR for the seed data.
  • Chipotle is bowl-assembly semantics; almost every other chain is item-purchase semantics — schema implication for menu_items in the supabase table.

Links

  • chipotle
  • brotein / bromenu / anabolic scoring
  • supabase
  • fast food nutrition dataset
  • restaurant menu scraping
